---
artifact_id: ESE111-CH04-PLAN
pair_id: ESE111-CH04-PLAN
lang: en
pair_revision: 0.1.0
semantic_id: ESE111-CH04-PLAN
id: ESE111-CH04-PLAN-en
resource_type: lecture-studio-plan
language: en
paired_with: ESE111-CH04-PLAN-fr
version: 0.1.0
lifecycle: drafting
duration_min: 90
outcomes: [ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
technical_contract:
  outcomes: [ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
  equations: [KCL signed current sum equals zero, KVL signed loop voltage sum equals zero, v equals R times i, power equals v times i]
  values: {source_v: 6.0, r1_ohm: 10000, r2_ohm: 22000, r3_ohm: 33000, r4_baseline_ohm: 15000, r4_redesign_ohm: 22000}
  safety_limits: {source_max_v: 6.1, current_limit_max_a: 0.02, dmm_current_input_permitted: false}
  expected_ranges: {baseline_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.087, 1.155], redesign_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.412, 1.500], source_current_simulated_a: [0.000229, 0.000244]}
  rubric_total: 28
  answer_key_digest: pending-restricted-store
  figure_ids: [ESE111-CH04-FIG-001]
---

# Studio plan — Every branch must appear in the evidence

## Intent and preparation

The target misconception change is from “current is consumed / measuring is
passive / debug by replacement” to signed conservation, instrument-as-branch, and
hypothesis-led testing. Collect the initial prediction, two faded derivation steps,
demo decision, fault-test vote, design choice, and individual exit ticket.

Before class: review chapter/claims/model; print slides, schematic, prepared table,
and exit slips; test the static route. A live demo requires its own current physical
pilot, L1 activity approval, function-check records, 15-minute setup, 5-minute reset,
and approved supervision. If any is missing, use the calculated/static fallback.
Provide paper, keyboard, oral, and partner-mediated response routes without changing
the individual reasoning threshold.

## Timed sequence

| Min | Phase | Learner action | Prompt/evidence | If evidence shows… |
|---:|---|---|---|---|
| 0–4 | Discover | choose first diagnostic move | anonymous prediction + confidence | replacement first → keep for later comparison |
| 4–9 | Retrieve | draw reference, polarity, arrow | individual sketch | unsafe DMM/reference gap → REM-dmm/reference |
| 9–15 | Discover | rank nodes/currents | poll then pair explanation | “used up” → ask where difference could accumulate |
| 15–31 | Understand | supply terms and eliminate | board equation + faded step | term lacks branch → trace schematic physically |
| 31–45 | Understand | reject result by estimate; check solution | bounds, KCL, loops, power | calculator-only → require independent check |
| 45–50 | Understand | name a model limit | one-sentence transfer | earth/COM confusion → explicit contrast |
| 50–60 | Experiment | predict loading; inspect static/live result | before/after pair | “bad meter” → add (R_M) rather than blame |
| 60–67 | Analyze | rank residual causes | mini uncertainty table | deletes anomaly → preserve original row |
| 67–76 | Analyze | choose test and predicted outcomes | fault decision vote | replacement/no outcomes → REM-debug |
| 76–84 | Design | choose R4 with margin | four-candidate trade study | nominal only → add corner/loading condition |
| 84–90 | Exit | revise model, disconfirm, limit claim | individual ticket | route by published classification |

## Board plan

Use four persistent zones. (1) Draw topology and text-only reading; name VS, A, B,
COM. (2) Add arrows/polarities and the estimate box. (3) Build KCL-A and KCL-B one
branch at a time, solve, then retain KCL/KVL/power checks. (4) Draw a residual table,
add (R_M) as a visibly new branch, then finish with the fault hypotheses and R4
requirements. Do not erase the prediction; mark what evidence changed it.

## Essential versus compressible

Never cut the prediction, reference/sign declaration, branch-to-equation mapping,
instrument branch, discriminating test, design acceptance, safety boundary, or exit
ticket. If time is short, provide the arithmetic solution and reduce historical
context, repeated calculations, and enrichment. Record actual timing, participation,
access barriers, ambiguous wording, apparatus failure, exit distribution, and owned
changes immediately after the session.
